title: Method of making spheroidal particles
abstract: A method for forming spheroidal particles including inducing flow of a slurry of particles and a reactant through one or more orifices, detaching an amount of the slurry from the slurry flow following exit from the one or more orifices, the detached amount forming a slurry body, forming the slurry body into a spheroidal shape, contacting the spheroidally shaped slurry body with a coagulation solution to form a stabilized spheroidal particle and drying and/or sintering the stabilized spheroidal particle.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for forming spheroidal particles comprising: inducing flow of a slurry comprised of particles and a reactant through one or more orifices; detaching an amount of the slurry from the slurry flow following exit from the one or more orifices, the detached amount forming a slurry body; forming the slurry body into a spheroidal shape; contacting the spheroidally shaped slurry body with a coagulation solution to form a stabilized spheroidal particle; and drying and/or sintering the stabilized spheroidal particle. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the inducing flow of the slurry is performed by applying a load to a piston in a container housing the slurry to force the slurry through the one or more orifices associated with an exit of the container, increasing pressure in the container housing the slurry to force the slurry through the one or more orifices associated with an exit of the container, decreasing a volume of the container housing the slurry to force the slurry through the one or more orifices associated with an exit of the container, or pumping the slurry from the container housing the slurry to force the slurry through the one or more orifices associated with an exit of the container. 3. The method according to claim 1, wherein the method further comprises forming the slurry prior to inducing flow of the slurry by mixing the reactant and the ceramic particles. 4. The method according to claim 1, wherein the detaching comprises controlling a flow rate of the slurry to permit the detaching to occur as a result of a weight of the amount of the slurry exited from the one or more orifices. 5. The method according to claim 1, wherein the detaching comprises applying a vibration energy to the one or more orifices to separate the amount of the slurry from the slurry flow. 6. The method according to claim 1, wherein the forming the slurry body into a spheroidal shape comprises having the slurry body fall a height between the one or more orifices and the coagulation solution. 7. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ceramic particles are alumina- containing particles. 8. The method according to claim 1, wherein the reactant is an alginate and the coagulation solution comprises a calcium salt. 9. The method according to claim 1, wherein the one or more orifices comprise one or more nozzles or one or more openings of a membrane. 10. The method according to claim 1, wherein the spheroidal particles are oblate spheroids having an average aspect ratio of 0.91 or less. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein the oblate spheroidal particles have an average aspect ratio of 0.8 or less. 12. The method according to claim 1, wherein the spheroidal particles are prolate spheroids having an average aspect ratio of 1.1 or more. 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein the prolate spheroidal particles have an average aspect ratio of 1.4 or more. 14. The method according to claim 1, wherein the spheroidal particles are controlled to have an oblate shape through control of a flow rate of the slurry through the one or more orifices and a distance between the one or more orifices and the coagulation solution. 15. The method according to claim 1, wherein the spheroidal particles are controlled to have an oblate shape or a prolate shape through control of a viscosity of the slurry. 16. The method according to claim 15, wherein the spheroidal particles are controlled to have an oblate shape through the control of the vibration energy used to severe the slurry body. 17. A treatment fluid comprising a viscous fluid and the spheroidal particles made by the method of claim 1. 18. The treatment fluid according to claim 17, further comprising fibers. 19. The treatment fluid according to claim 17, further comprising substantially spherical proppant particles.
